topic: New Jerusalem pole axis — eschatological claim
change_summary: Since c2 (2026-04-07), WIN-032 was substantially rewritten per the c2 recommendations. claim shortened 92→23 chars, finding shortened 148→50 chars, detail_evidence expanded 621→1226 chars (+97%), detail_verdict_text expanded 81→1526 chars (+1784%, driven by addition of a second paragraph framing WIN-032 as failing the post-hoc standard). New text differentiates WIN-032 from WIN-031, notes that the pole-axis location is not in Revelation 21, emphasizes monitor.py absence, and mentions the geopolitical framing in context.html. Verdict remains Unfalsifiable.
our_argument_summary: Our review classifies WIN-032 Unfalsifiable — a theological assertion (New Jerusalem descending along the dome's north pole axis) with no measurable physical content. The current text argues: (1) this is biblical exegesis, not physical hypothesis; (2) the dome's own automated monitoring system has no domain for WIN-032; (3) WIN-032 is doubly unfalsifiable relative to WIN-031 (requires both dome geometry and prophetic framework); (4) the pole-axis location is not in Revelation 21 but was added by the dome author; (5) the geopolitical framing in context.html confirms this is a theological-political claim; (6) counting theological WINs alongside empirical ones inflates the scorecard. A second detail_verdict paragraph argues the prediction/retrodiction distinction is moot because the claim has no observable to pre-register.

relevant_files: raw-text/05-model.txt line 12 — lists New Jerusalem descending along pole axis (WIN-032) as an architectural element
raw-text/02-wins.txt line 117 — lists WIN-032 — New Jerusalem North Pole Axis CONFIRMED with no elaboration
raw-text/08-live-power.txt — the V51.0 live dashboard enumerates 20 monitoring domains; WIN-032 is absent from all of them
raw-text/07-ai-context.txt — frames the pole axis within an eschatological-geopolitical narrative (Temple Mount, control point)
monitor.py — referenced in dome documentation as the automated monitoring system; not in our raw-text archive, but the live dashboard (20 domains) contains no WIN-032 test
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: None. No formula, data fetch, or pass/fail check exists for WIN-032 anywhere in the dome's automation. The 20-domain live dashboard (Schumann, Tesla longitudinal, NMP drift, equatorial gravity, EM-gravity coupling, aetheric slipstream, GPS Sagnac, lunar magnetic tide, etc.) has no slot that could meaningfully test the descent of a supernatural city along a geometric axis. The dome site displays WIN-032 as CONFIRMED purely via static HTML.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 100% static assertion. No computation, no data pipeline, no validation logic.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None applicable — with no measurable claim, there is nothing to escape from. The claim is structured to be permanently future-tensed.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our detail_evidence says monitor.py has a 39-domain audit engine. The 39 appears to come from V50.6's count of confirmed WINs (raw-text/02-wins.txt: Version 50.6, 2026-03-12, 39 confirmed, 0 falsified and Confirmed Wins (39)). The live monitoring dashboard at V51.0 is described as a 20-Domain Convergence Dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt). Our text conflates the count of confirmed WINs (39 in V50.6) with the count of monitoring domains (20 in V51.0). The substantive claim — that WIN-032 is absent from the dome's monitoring automation — is still correct, but the specific number is wrong. Recommend replacing 39-domain audit engine with either 20-domain convergence dashboard or the neutral phrasing the dome's automated monitoring pipeline has no validation domain for WIN-032.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Verified: raw-text/08-live-power.txt enumerates all 20 monitoring domains in V51.0, and none of them test or reference WIN-032. The claim is purely displayed as static text in the WINs scorecard (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 117). No API fetch, no formula, no tolerance check.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics has no account of a supernatural city descending along a geometric axis — there is nothing to relabel. This is pure eschatology appended to dome geometry.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Revelation 21 (c. 95–96 AD) long predates the dome model. The dome adopts Revelation's New Jerusalem as an existing prediction and claims the dome geometry specifies the location (pole axis). Revelation itself does not specify a geographic location — the pole-axis interpretation was added by the dome author. This is retrodiction dressed as prediction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: There is no mathematical derivation from dome parameters (disc_radius, firmament_height, etc.) to New Jerusalem descends along the pole axis. It is an interpretive assertion. The only geometric fact used — that the dome has a single symmetry axis — is a shared feature of any axially symmetric model.

holes_found.description: Hardcoded stale number: detail_evidence says at least 4 theological claims in the 67 that carry zero empirical content yet appear in the denominator of the 95.2% accuracy figure. Both numbers are stale. Current wins.json has 71 WINs, not 67. And the dome's current claimed-accuracy figure (per data/uncounted-failures.json dome_claimed_accuracy) is 94.5%, not 95.2%. CLAUDE.md rule: never hardcode a number that can be derived from the data. We criticize the dome for exactly this; we must not do it ourselves.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace with templated counts computed at build time (e.g., {{TOTAL_WINS}} and {{DOME_CLAIMED_ACCURACY}}), or re-phrase to avoid specific numbers: This is one of the four theological assertions that carry zero empirical content yet sit in the denominator of the dome author's claimed accuracy figure.

holes_found.description: Factual slippage: detail_evidence refers to monitor.py's 39-domain audit engine. The V51.0 live dashboard is described as a 20-Domain Convergence Dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 11). The 39 figure comes from V50.6's count of confirmed WINs, not the count of monitoring domains. A dome defender could point to this to discredit the surrounding argument, even though the core claim (WIN-032 is not monitored) is correct.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 39-domain audit engine with 20-domain convergence dashboard or the version-agnostic phrasing the dome's automated monitoring pipeline has no validation domain for WIN-032. The argument does not depend on the specific number of monitored domains.

holes_found.description: detail_verdict_text repeats The dome's own monitoring system excludes WIN-032 and monitor.py contains no validation domain for this WIN — these are essentially the same point restated two paragraphs apart. The second paragraph was added to connect WIN-032 to the #posthoc-standard tab, but the restated monitor.py sentence is now redundant.
holes_found.recommendation: Drop the second paragraph's The dome's own monitoring system (monitor.py) contains no validation domain for this WIN sentence, since the first paragraph already makes this point.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence says This WIN exemplifies at least 4 theological claims in the 67 that carry zero empirical content but does not list them. A reader without prior context cannot verify the 4 figure or connect it to Pattern #6 / Scorecard-Inflation arguments elsewhere in the review.
holes_found.recommendation: Either enumerate the four (WIN-023, WIN-031, WIN-032, WIN-034) inline, or anchor-link to the section that discusses the scorecard-inflation pattern.

holes_found.description: The phrase doubly unfalsifiable, as it requires both accepting the dome geometry and the prophetic framework is a vivid rhetorical point but compresses two distinct epistemic conditions. A dome defender could argue unfalsifiability is a property of the claim, not a degree — either something has a falsification criterion or it does not. Calling it doubly unfalsifiable invites this rebuttal.
holes_found.recommendation: Either recast as compounds two unfalsifiable layers: the dome geometry and the prophetic framework (keeping the rhetorical force without implying degrees of unfalsifiability), or acknowledge briefly that unfalsifiability is binary and explain what the doubly metaphor means operationally.

stronger_arguments.description: The CONFIRMED label itself is the cleanest target. Confirmation in science requires that a claim could have been refuted by measurement but was not. WIN-032 has no measurement, so the word CONFIRMED is a category error regardless of whether one accepts the prophecy. This reframing does not require any comparison to monitor.py or other WINs — the label cannot attach to an untestable claim, period.
sources: raw-text/02-wins.txt line 117 — WIN-032 — New Jerusalem North Pole Axis CONFIRMED with no elaboration or falsification criterion

stronger_arguments.description: The Revelation passage does not pin the New Jerusalem to any pole or axis. Revelation 21:2 describes the city coming down out of heaven from God as a four-square city of measured dimensions (12,000 stadia per side) — a geometric specification but no geographic coordinate. The dome author has added the pole-axis location. This addition-without-attribution is the post-hoc tell; it is worth calling out explicitly with the verse reference.
sources: Revelation 21:2, 21:16 (city laid out as a square of 12,000 stadia; no axis specified)

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's monitoring dashboard exposes the boundary: its 20 domains are all present-tense measurable (Schumann, NMP drift, equatorial gravity, eclipse, GPS Sagnac, etc.). That boundary is exactly where WIN-032 falls outside. The dome author has tacitly accepted this boundary by building the dashboard around testable claims and leaving WIN-032 out; our review should name this pattern explicitly rather than leaving it implicit.

kernel_of_truth.description: Integrating cosmology with prophetic texts is a coherent intellectual project in theology (e.g., medieval cathedral architecture encoded cosmic order; modern process theology attempts similar integration). Identifying the dome's single symmetry axis as the privileged locus for eschatological events is internally consistent with the dome's architecture.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Internal consistency is necessary but not sufficient. A claim can be internally coherent and still fail the requirements for empirical confirmation: (1) there is no measurement that could distinguish worlds where WIN-032 is true from worlds where it is false; (2) the claim is future-tensed with no specified timeline; (3) the pole-axis specification is not in Revelation but is the dome author's addition; (4) the dome's own monitoring pipeline excludes it. Labeling an internally-consistent theological claim CONFIRMED alongside measured magnetic anomalies is a category error, not a validated prediction.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome advocate might argue: Your review is internally inconsistent. You demand falsification criteria for WIN-032 but not for standard-model claims about the pre-CMB universe, the interior of black holes, or string-theoretic vacua — all of which are currently untestable. You exempt standard physics from the unfalsifiability test and apply it only to our theological claims. That is not methodological rigor; it is bias against a faith-based model. Moreover, the dome model is explicit about integrating physics with prophetic texts — we never pretended WIN-032 was a measurement; the CONFIRMED label applies because the prophecy is consistent with dome geometry, which is a weaker but legitimate form of validation within our own framework. If you accept our framework, the claim follows; if you reject it, the claim was never offered to you. You are rejecting the wrapper and pretending you have refuted the content.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Preempt this in detail_verdict_text by naming the move: the dome does not get to invent a weaker 'consistent with model' meaning of CONFIRMED for claims it cannot measure, and still put that claim in the same scorecard as empirical measurements with falsification thresholds. Either the CONFIRMED label has one meaning across all WINs — in which case WIN-032 does not qualify — or the dome must separate scorecards (empirical vs theological). Our Scorecard-Inflation / Pattern 6 argument depends on holding this line. A short addition such as: The dome author is free to define CONFIRMED as consistent with the model's theological wrapper — but that definition cannot coexist in the same scorecard with Schumann-resonance measurements and magnetic-field anomalies that have explicit falsification thresholds. Mixing the two is the category error; the fix is not to relitigate the theology but to insist on a single definition of confirmation.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: All four theological WINs (023: SAA formation ~950 AD / Satan's little season; 031: North Pole Cosmic Mountain; 032: New Jerusalem pole axis; 034: Firmament = cast copper/bronze) carry the Unfalsifiable verdict in current wins.json. Our findings across these four are compatible: all note absence from monitor.py, all call out post-hoc retrofitting of ancient texts to dome geometry, and all are counted toward the scorecard-inflation pattern. One minor rhetorical inconsistency worth noting: WIN-032's detail_evidence calls this doubly unfalsifiable while WIN-031's finding treats unfalsifiability as a binary property (no measurable physical consequence distinguishes this from any cosmology with a North Pole axis). The arguments do not contradict each other but the rhetorical framing differs; a defender could pick at this. Low priority — easily fixed in a sweep.

95.2% accuracy figure — current data/uncounted-failures.json lists dome_claimed_accuracy as 94.5%, with variants [96.3%, 100.0%, 89.3%, 94.7%]; 95.2% is not among them and appears to be stale

quantitative_verification.computations: Counted theological WINs in data/wins.json by scanning verdict=Unfalsifiable entries and filtering for biblical/prophetic content: 4 matches (IDs 023, 031, 032, 034). Confirmed total WIN count via JSON.parse(wins.json).length = 71. Pulled current dome_claimed_accuracy from data/uncounted-failures.json = 94.5%. Enumerated live dashboard domains from raw-text/08-live-power.txt: 20 distinct per-domain sections.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Two stale hardcoded numbers in detail_evidence (67 and 95.2%) and one stale/confused count (39-domain audit engine). See holes_found entries #1 and #2.

deeper_analysis: Cycle 3 is primarily a change-confirmation review. The c2 pass recommended a major_edit with seven specific holes, and the rewrite addressed most of them: claim and finding were tightened (the c2 text had a mini-essay in the claim field; current text is concise); detail_evidence was expanded with the monitor.py argument, WIN-031 differentiation, the pole-axis-not-in-Revelation point, and the scorecard-inflation framing; detail_verdict_text gained a second paragraph tying WIN-032 to the post-hoc standard and clarifying why the prediction/retrodiction distinction is moot here. The verdict Unfalsifiable still holds and the argumentation is now much sharper than in cycle 2. The advocate_mode check found one defense (the 'you exempt standard physics from unfalsifiability' move) that rates a 3 — plausible enough to merit preemptive neutralization, since a reader encountering it unguarded might think we are applying an inconsistent standard. The preemptive fix is to name the move: the dome cannot use a looser definition of CONFIRMED for its theological claims while keeping them in the same scorecard as claims with explicit falsification thresholds. Cross-WIN consistency with WIN-023, WIN-031, WIN-034 is good — all four carry Unfalsifiable verdicts with compatible arguments. Quantitative verification surfaced two stale hardcoded numbers (67 and 95.2%) and one confused count (39-domain audit engine). These are the clearest holes: 67 is now 71; 95.2% is not the dome's current claimed accuracy (94.5% is); 39 matches V50.6's confirmed-WIN count, not V51.0's 20 monitoring domains. Per CLAUDE.md, numbers that can be derived from data must not be hardcoded — we criticize the dome for doing this, so we cannot do it ourselves. Replacing the hardcoded numbers with templated values (or more durable qualitative phrasing) would close the last two significant vulnerabilities in this WIN's text. Recommendation: minor_edit. The verdict stands; the argument is solid; the only remaining repairs are stale-number fixes and the two suggested rhetorical tightenings.
